In the outer corners beginning with the north-east of a
house there dwell evil spirits viz. Caraki, Vidari, Putana and Raksasi
respectively. Hence those who dwell in these corners of cities,
houses and villages come to grief, whereas the outcastes such as
Candalas (who eat dog's meat) alone flourish there.
[Cf. what other works say :

Among the outcastes are included cobblers, Pukkasas etc. Pukkasa
is the offspring of a Nisada by a 8udra woman. (Manu X. 18).]

The trees Peepal, banyan, the Indian fig and the holy A^vat-
tha growing in the south, west, north and east respectively are of
untoward effects; while in the north, east, south and west respec-
tively they are beneficial.
